danger_signals.visual_form: Thematic hazards matching boss weapon type (heat, ice, wind, water, etc.)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger scaled to boss threat level; Air Man's sky stage features wind hazards; Heat Man's stage features fire obstacles

danger_signals.visual_form: Small robotic enemies with distinct animations and trajectories
danger_signals.meaning: Minion threats that telegraph attack patterns through movement; hitbox danger communicated through sprite boundaries

danger_signals.visual_form: Industrial, mechanical environment with spikes, platforms, and moving obstacles
danger_signals.meaning: Final danger zone; escalated threat level signaled through denser hazard placement

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct tileset graphics showing obvious hazard geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Instant-death or damage threats; visual clarity prevents unfair punishment

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible item icon dropped by bosses after defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; signals progression checkpoint and health resource abundance

safety_signals.visual_form: Unique floating platform objects granted after each Robot Master defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Access to previously unreachable safe zones; tactical progression signal

safety_signals.visual_form: Solid terrain without animated hazards or destructible elements
safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal space; rest areas between combat encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: UI display with eight boss portraits arranged in grid
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and safety; ability to choose order of engagement reduces forced progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Robot Master designs (8 distinct mechanical enemy types); Wily fortress architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Eight Robot Masters each with unique visual design reflecting their weapon theme: Metal Man (metallic silver), Air Man (aerodynamic blue), Bubble Man (aquatic blue-green), Quick Man (sleek yellow), Crash Man (heavy brown), Flash Man (gold/bright yellow), Heat Man (red/orange), Wood Man (green/brown)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress stages feature industrial aesthetic with mechanical hazards; stage themes directly reflect boss weapon functionality

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Humanoid robot design with distinctive helmet and arm cannon
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dr. Light's lab implied as safe zone; no explicit environmental faction markers beyond stage progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Creator/sender of Mega Man; referenced in plot but minimal visual representation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Implicit starting point; mission briefing authority

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen with stage select menu showing 8 unopened Robot Master stages; all stages available with no visual progression indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can select any stage in any order; no gating mechanism; complete strategic freedom

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage select menu updates to show defeated boss (crossed out or dimmed visual); weapon becomes available in HUD; new special platform item appears in levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Previously impassable areas become accessible via new weapon or platform tool; difficulty curve adjusted through player choice of next stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage select transitions to Wily fortress levels; new tileset aesthetic (industrial fortress); escalated visual complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Linear progression enforced; no stage select option; increased enemy density and hazard complexity; rematch encounters with Robot Masters in single rooms

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wily Machine visual design; mechanical amalgamation showing threat escalation; holographic alien holdup variant appears mid-fight
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Distinct multi-phase fight pattern; Wily attempts deception with alien impersonation; mercy mechanic available upon victory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Password display screen after each Robot Master defeat; cryptographic character grid shown
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game state persistently saveable without continuous play; allows non-linear return to checkpoint progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Heat Man's stage features fire/lava obstacles; Air Man's stage is set in sky with wind hazards; Bubble Man's stage contains water mechanics and aquatic aesthetic. Thematic coherence communicates boss domain and weapon utility.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Robot Master visual design reflecting function
environmental_storytelling.example: Metal Man has metallic appearance suggesting durability; Air Man has aerodynamic silhouette; Wood Man features organic vegetation. Visual design predicts weapon type and stage environment without explicit UI.

Final fortress levels use denser tileset patterns, more animated hazards, and industrial architecture compared to thematic Robot Master stages. Visual progression from diverse themed areas to unified fortress communicates narrative climax.

Different enemy types populate different stages; Friender enemies in specific zones; Acid enemy in Wily Stage 6. Enemy sprite variation communicates environmental faction control and danger diversity.
Energy Tanks and special platform items appear as rewards, not pickup litter. Strategic placement guides player attention to accomplishment moments and safe resource availability.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fires metal blade projectiles in predictable arcs; movement pattern telegraphs attack timing; weak to Crash Man's weapon
Rapid movement with jumping pattern; can block projectiles before launching attacks; single-frame standing sprite during block recovery indicates vulnerability window
Uses time-freeze weapon on Mega Man; no mid-air shooting behavior in final game (despite unused sprite); stage features light-based hazards matching weapon theme
Operates Wily Machine with ball projectile; attacks from mechanical platform; malfunctioning device shows damage progression
Attempts holographic alien disguise; alien sprite differs from robot master designs; hologram failure reveals Wily at controls; mercy trigger activated upon defeat
Static or looping movement pattern; front paw animation unused in final game (sprite detail removed); telegraphs threat through animation clarity
Arm cannon visual changes to reflect active weapon; HUD displays current weapon selection; fire pattern varies by equipped weapon type

notes: Mega Man 2 communicates world-state primarily through stage selection freedom and progressive environmental complexity. The game uses thematic coherence (boss weapon type = stage environment type) as core storytelling method. Faction identity is minimal—Dr. Wily's threat is expressed through his Robot Masters' distinct visual designs and stage domination rather than environmental faction markers. The password system creates visual progression checkpoints. Safety is signaled through resource item placement (Energy Tanks) and player agency (stage select). Danger manifests through environmental hazards matched to thematic context and enemy behavior patterns. The final fortress represents world-state shift from choice-based to linear narrative progression, visually communicated through aesthetic escalation and enemy density increase. Holographic alien deception in final boss shows narrative complexity through sprite substitution mechanics.
